<!-- llms.txt — describes unify-kit itself; sourced from templates/llms.txt.template's pattern-only shape. -->

# unify-kit

> A reusable, opinionated kickstarter for any new Claude Code project. The Ultimate Guide teaches; this kit ships.

## What this is

Unify-kit is a starter kit that *uses* Claude Code well. It composes existing plugins (`superpowers`) with a curated set of templates, hooks, GitHub Actions, scripts, and onboarding docs that encode mature practice. Goal: time-to-productivity. Clone the repo, run `/plugin install unifylabs-workflow` then `scripts/init-project.sh`, and a new project (or new dev) lands on day one with a team-ready `CLAUDE.md`, seven security hooks (+ context-awareness UX hook = 8 total) installed, a comment-triggered PR-review workflow wired up, and a short onboarding curriculum.

## Stack

- Bash + macOS/Linux only (Windows users use WSL)
- GitHub Actions for the kit's own CI and the consumer-shipped review workflow
- Markdown + JSON for kit-shipped artifacts
- `jq` for JSON manipulation in `init-project.sh`

## Key directories

- `templates/` — 7 templates + 4 Next.js snippets + README; `cheatsheet.md.template` is the source of truth for command vocabulary and reviewer-agent mapping
- `plugins/unifylabs-workflow/hooks/` — 8 hooks (7 security + context-awareness) + `settings-snippet.json` + README
- `scripts/` — `init-project.sh` + `audit-scan.sh` + `dev-symlink-skills.sh` + test fixtures
- `github-actions/` — comment-triggered tiered PR-review workflow + externalized prompt + adoption README
- `docs/` — `philosophy.md` (5 principles) + `methodology.md` (operational canon) + `decisions/` (ADRs)
- `onboarding/` — day-1 / week-1 / day-30 curriculum
- `specs/` — pre-implementation specs (historical record)

## Key conventions

- Placeholder syntax: `{{NAME}}` — single mandatory convention; the literal `<...>` is reserved for prose
- Filename canon: lowercase-hyphenated; `.md.template` for editable, `.md` for lift-as-is
- Sourcing modes: `verbatim` / `verbatim-with-light-edit` / `customization` / `pattern-only` — every reuse is tagged
- Hierarchy of authority: `<consumer>/CLAUDE.md` > `docs/methodology.md` > plugin defaults > Claude Code defaults
- Spec-first contribution flow: open issue → write numbered spec → review → implement
- CHANGELOG discipline: per-PR `[Unreleased]` entries enforced by `.github/workflows/changelog-check.yml`
- Living docs on every ship: the project's living-doc set is named in `<consumer>/CLAUDE.md`

## How to ask for help

- For consumer adoption: read `README.md` → `/plugin install unifylabs-workflow` → run `scripts/init-project.sh` → copy templates from `templates/`
- For contribution: read `CONTRIBUTING.md` → open an issue → propose a spec under `specs/` → implement
- For methodology / philosophy: read `docs/philosophy.md` (5 principles) and `docs/methodology.md` (operational canon)
- For onboarding: read `onboarding/day-1.md` → `onboarding/week-1.md` → `onboarding/day-30.md`

## License

MIT (code) + CC0 1.0 (templates) + CC BY-SA 4.0 (narrative docs). See `LICENSE`.

## Status

v2.0.3 — current release. Ships as the `unifylabs-workflow` plugin (`/plugin install unifylabs-workflow`) plus `scripts/init-project.sh` for per-project scaffolding. Follow-up items are tracked in `BACKLOG.md`.
